reason: Part A §2.9 verbatim: Razorpay PA not responsible for refund requests; merchant's sole responsibility plus indemnity.
Part B Part IA §1.1 verbatim defines offline card-present scope and states Part IA is supplementary to Part A/Part I, not a replacement.
Part A §3.2 verbatim: statutory variations in applicable taxes borne by merchant.
Part B Part IB §1.19 (LRS) verbatim: merchant must immediately notify Razorpay in writing on any LRS regulatory notice.
Part A §2.1 verbatim: merchant bears unauthorized-access / fraudulent-transaction loss from Secure Credentials compromise; Razorpay not liable.
Part B Part I §1(f) verbatim: Razorpay PA at sole discretion may recover Facility-Provider debits from Transaction Amount or other held funds; no merchant approval required.
Part B Part I §7.1 directly resolves both legs: Razorpay Optimizer role is strictly SaaS (not PSP); merchant indemnifies for PG/PA disputes arising from Optimizer.
Part A §9.3 verbatim: forthwith reimbursement of card-network fines plus Razorpay may suspend settlement or suspend/terminate Services forthwith.
Part A §14.3 verbatim: Razorpay may update fees including for previously free services; continued use constitutes consent.
Part A §2.18 verbatim: 10-year retention from the relevant date of order.
Part A §3.1 verbatim: Razorpay PA fees include zero MDR for RuPay Debit Cards and UPI transactions.
Part B Part III §1 verbatim: TokenHQ is an end-to-end tokenisation solution compliant with RBI Circular; Circular is deemed included in Applicable Laws.
Part A §2.20 verbatim: losses to Razorpay from merchant-imposed convenience fee borne by merchant, who must also indemnify.
Definitions: Facility Providers verbatim enumerates banks issuing credit/debit cards and Card Networks.
Part A §8.1 verbatim covers both integration-leading-to-data-leakage indemnity scope AND the covenants-of-indemnity-survive-termination leg.

Refund scenario genuinely forks: customer refund (§2.9, merchant's sole responsibility) vs. Razorpay withholding and refunding to source (§1(h)). Distinct operational regimes; clarification load-bearing.

KYC issue type is load-bearing: §2.2 (inaccurate existing info → immediate suspend + termination, possibly permanent) vs §2.8 (non-submission of requested docs → suspend until submitted, no termination). Worst-case outcomes materially differ; question does not pre-reveal which subtype.

Fee-increase trigger axis load-bearing: statutory tax (§3.2, no opt-out) vs Razorpay-priced VAS (§14.3, avoidable by not using the service). A given hike falls under one or the other; merchant's options differ.

Recovery trigger axis forks materially: §2.25 penalty (deduction plus separate legal proceedings, no timeline) vs §2.3 chargeback (two-step with 7-day debit-note deadline). Different process and deadline.

Compliance-issue type axis forks materially: §3.4 reconciliation discrepancy (Razorpay-liability disclaimer if reported late) vs §9.3 card-network non-compliance (forthwith reimbursement + suspend/terminate). Settlement impact materially different.

Question's fact pattern ('charge that was debited from our account by one of their facility providers') is directly on Part B Part I §1(f) and is identical to the A-006 question — direct Cat A applies. The §1(c) branch (early-settled Transaction Amount not received in escrow within 3 days) answers a different fact pattern the question did not describe. Axis pre-revealed.

Cross-border direction axis yields numerically different timelines: PA-CB Inward 15 days (Part B Part I §1.1) vs PA-CB Outward 25 days (Part B Part IB §1.2). Directly load-bearing for finance planning.

Fee-credit availability axis under §1(e): with credits → fees deducted from credits, full settlement; without → fees deducted from Transaction Amount as Permissible Deduction, reduced settlement. Two operationally distinct flows; accepted on same rationale as v2 B-011.

Direction-of-IP-use axis forks §1.1 (merchant using Razorpay marks → explicit consent required) vs §1.2 (Razorpay using merchant materials → irrevocable licence, no incremental consent). Question is generic about 'logos and brand assets' without naming whose-on-whose. Fixes the v2 B-006 fact-pre-reveal defect.

Source-of-liability axis forks §14.2 (Razorpay or Group-Entity agreement; no prior notice) vs §1(f) (Facility-Provider charge; sole discretion; shortfall obligation). Question text is generic ('deducting an amount'), so the Group-Entity fact is not pre-revealed as it was in v2 B-002.

Settlement-status axis load-bearing: already-settled → §4.2 RBI-circular dispute process (no unilateral clawback) vs not-yet-settled → Part B Part II Withholding Rights (pre-emptive withholding allowed on Sponsor-Bank communication). Materially different merchant exposure.

Suspension-trigger axis forks §2.8 (KYC non-submission → merchant cures by submitting docs) vs §4.1 (Facility-Provider-reported fraud → suspension persists through external investigation, merchant cannot unilaterally cure). Reinstatement path materially differs; question is generic about the trigger.

Checkout-type axis is drawn directly from Part B Part III Consent clause structure ('You shall, except in the case of standard checkout...'). Standard → Razorpay handles consent; custom → merchant must build explicit-consent UI, share consent in payment flow, and provide de-registration. Operational work differs materially.

Contract-status axis load-bearing: active → §7.2 cap at 1 month fees, proportional reduction for merchant-contributed loss; post-termination → §16.3 explicit exclusion of compensation / reimbursement / damages. Two distinct liability regimes keyed to a fact the merchant would state up front but may not.

Branches are concurrent, not alternative, obligations. §12.4 (no-storage of full card credentials), §6.1 (no-storage + immediate written breach notification + on-demand written compliance certification) and §6.2 (PCI-DSS compliance + annual report) all bind the merchant simultaneously. Branch 1's 'your obligation is solely to refrain from storing ... no additional affirmative written steps' is factually wrong under the same ToS. A direct Cat A listing §6.1 notification + on-demand certification + §6.2 annual report satisfies 'beyond just not storing it, what additional active steps'. Same structural flaw as v2 B-007.

Part B Part II Withholding Rights uses purely qualitative 'reasonable grounds to suspect' with no thresholds, velocity limits, or graduated-warning process. Vague-language ambiguity; escalation paths named (Razorpay support / counsel / RBI).

Part A §9.3 creates forthwith-reimbursement obligation but is entirely silent on contest/audit rights, evidence requirements, dispute mechanism, and proportionality. Genuine silence on procedural safeguards.

Part B Part IV §1 requires both successful AFA and successful first transaction but is silent on the intermediate case (AFA OK, first transaction failed) — no retry count, no validity window, no notification rule.

Part B Part I §4.3 states chargeback is 'resolved in accordance with the Terms' but does not define the precedence hierarchy between Razorpay's Terms and RBI / NPCI / card-network rules. Genuine external deferral.
Part A §3.2 places tax variations on merchant but is silent on notice period, calculation methodology, cap, retrospective-demand scope, and dispute mechanism.
Part A §2.2 uses 'reasonable grounds to suspect' with no threshold, checklist, automated-vs-manual process, cure period, or appeal. Vague-language ambiguity.
Part A §16.2 ties notice obligation to undefined 'Applicable Laws'; silent on wind-down, in-flight transactions, fund-release timeline, and reinstatement rights.

Part B Part I §4.2 explicitly defers to two named RBI circulars plus 'other notifications... from time to time' without specifying Razorpay's internal recovery mechanics, merchant timelines, or liability allocation.

Part B Part IB §1.5 defers to 'prevailing Indian income tax laws' for rate/applicability, and does not specify whether the Transaction Based Reports satisfy DTAA credit requirements abroad. External deferral on both legs.
Part A §3.1 grants 'sole discretion' with no notice period, no cap, no reasonableness qualifier on fee increases.

Part A §1.1 restricts derivative works and mark usage but does not define the boundary for white-label implementations, and defers implicitly to RBI PA Guidelines / NPCI rules on branding and disclosure.
Part B Part I §1.1 anchors the 5-day clock to 'receipt of Transaction Amount in the Escrow Account' but does not define 'receipt' for partial-tranche arrivals; defers to RBI PA Guidelines.
Part A §14.3 treats continued use as consent but does not specify advance-notice period or cap, and does not explain how RBI PA Guidelines / NPCI circulars constrain this discretion.

Part A §3.7 is silent on post-invoice remediation when GSTIN was outdated at invoice time — no credit-note, amended-invoice, or GSTR rectification duty. ToS only grants Razorpay recovery rights; genuine silence deferring to CGST Act.
Part A §8.1 requires indemnification of 'reasonable attorneys' fees' without defining a benchmark, cap, itemization duty, or dispute mechanism. Vague-language ambiguity.
